_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including history, evaluation findings, vital monitoring, and planned line of management
Sepsis screening report such as complete blood count, C-reactive protein

claim.label: Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) (including any cross-specialty referral that has been done)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, vital monitoring, planned treatment line, and advice for admission?
Did the imaging ± investigations confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs (including any cross-specialty referral that has been done) with daily vitals and treatment details submitted?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Patient did not present with typical symptoms of acute abdomen (abdominal pain, distention, bloating, constipation, vomiting, diarrhea, dysentery).
Clinical presentation, imaging, or investigations failed to confirm the diagnosis of acute abdomen.
Clinical notes missing or incomplete, lacking detailed history, signs/symptoms, vital monitoring, planned treatment, or advice for admission.
Mandatory imaging (USG Abdomen / CT / Contrast X-rays) or investigation reports (e.g., Sepsis screening, Blood culture, Stool culture) not submitted at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) are incomplete or do not include daily vitals and treatment details at claim submission.
Discharge summary missing or incomplete, or does not include follow-up advice at discharge.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/DNB/DCH/Equivalent (Pediatric), DM/DNB/Equivalent (Gastroenterology), Surgical referral if required
clinical_key_pointers: Abdominal pain is a common problem in children. Although most children with acute abdominal pain have self-limited conditions, the pain may lead to surgical or medical emergency. Clinically, abdominal pain falls into 3 categories: Visceral Pain, Parietal Pain, Referred Pain.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Surgical referral if required; decide referral of cases to the appropriate level of care; consider surgical consultation.
